description: This Kotlin source file implements a mock backend server as an OkHttp interceptor. It simulates authentication endpoints (/auth/login, /auth/refresh) and a protected resource (/protected), managing tokens in-memory and responding with JSON payloads. The class is designed for demo purposes to allow running authentication flows without a real server.

classes.name: MockBackendInterceptor
classes.description: A class implementing an OkHttp Interceptor to simulate backend API endpoints for login, token refresh, and protected resources, using in-memory token storage and simple JSON responses for demo purposes.

methods.name: intercept
methods.description: Intercepts HTTP requests, and based on the request path and method, returns mock responses for login, refresh, or protected resource access.

methods.name: issueTokens
methods.description: Generates new access and refresh tokens, updates their state, and sets expiry time, returning a TokenResponse object.

methods.name: readUtf8Body
methods.description: Reads the UTF-8 string content from the request body.

methods.name: parseJsonField
methods.description: Parses a simple JSON string to extract the value of a specified field.

methods.name: tokenResponseJson
methods.description: Creates a JSON string representing the token response payload.

methods.name: jsonResponse
methods.description: Constructs an HTTP Response with given status code and JSON body.
